# USA v Bosnia and Herzegovina: Live stream, team news, tickets and more

Published26 Jun 2026

Share

Christian Pulisic is set to return to the starting line-up as the co-hosts target a place in the Round of 16 against Edin Dzeko and co.

## USA v Bosnia and HerzegovinaUSA v Bosnia and Herzegovina

https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/17/285023/289287/400021524 https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/17/285023/289287/400021524

### Round of 32Round of 32

Wednesday, 1 July | San Francisco Bay Area Stadium

#### Kick-off timeKick-off time

17:00 (San Francisco) | 02:00 2 July (Sarajevo)

---

### The matchThe match

USA are the third and final co-hosts in FIFA World Cup 2026™ Round of 32 action as Mauricio Pochettino's team face Bosnia and Herzegovina in San Francisco after winning Group D. The Stars and Stripes proved too strong for Paraguay and Australia, before a much-changed side lost a five-goal thriller to Türkiye in the closing seconds.

Bosnia and Herzegovina – who qualified for the finals after beating four-time world champions Italy in the UEFA play-offs – finished with four points in Gro

---

### The matchThe match

USA are the third and final co-hosts in FIFA World Cup 2026™ Round of 32 action as Mauricio Pochettino's team face Bosnia and Herzegovina in San Francisco after winning Group D. The Stars and Stripes proved too strong for Paraguay and Australia, before a much-changed side lost a five-goal thriller to Türkiye in the closing seconds.

Bosnia and Herzegovina – who qualified for the finals after beating four-time world champions Italy in the UEFA play-offs – finished with four points in Gro

news_items.title: United States vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch in UK, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN
news_items.author: Aaditya Narayan
news_items.text:
United States vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch in UK, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

The USA will look to lay another marker down on their home World Cup in Santa Clara on Wednesday night, when they face Bosnia and Herzegovina in the Round of 32.

Mauricio Pochettino's side won their group with 4-1 and 2-0 wins over Paraguay and Australia respectively in their first two games, which guaranteed that they'd top the group. Pochettino made a raft of changes for an inconsequential third group game against Turkiye, which the USA lost 3-2, after Kaan Ayhan scored the winner with the last kick of the game.

The likes of Tyler Adams, Folarin Balogun, and Chris Richards will return to the side for this game, as the USA look to book a Round of 16 tie against either Belgium or Senegal.

The Bosnians will be no pushovers though. They won one and drew one of the group games, either side of a big loss to Switzerland, but not long ago, they did eliminate Italy in the qualifiers to be a part of this World Cup. They will no doubt look to spoil this American party in Santa Clara, and

news_items.title: USMNT vs. Bosnia & Herzegovina—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups
news_items.text:
USMNT vs. Bosnia & Herzegovina—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups

The United States believes in its soccer team again, and Mauricio Pochettino’s team is out to further embolden American dreams when it faces Bosnia & Herzegovina in the round of 32 at this summer’s World Cup.

Anxiety permeated across the country in the lead-up to the USMNT’s opening group game against Paraguay, but fears of a home humiliation were rapidly dispelled. Instead of wilting, it rose to the occasion and produced the most exhilarating performance of Pochettino’s tenure to date.

A 2–0 win over the plucky Australia followed, guaranteeing top spot in Group D with a game to play. That’s why the USMNT manager went on the defensive in the wake of the last-gasp defeat to Türkiye, with Pochettino opting for wholesale rotation to prepare for the knockout stages.

The U.S. must now bypass European opposition to prolong their World Cup journey, having historically struggled against UEFA members. Bosnia & Herzegovina is far from a juggernaut, but it’s a tall and physical team that has dominated from set-pieces at the tournament so far.

A 3–1 victory over Qatar on Matchday 3 saw Bosnia into the knockouts as

news_items.title: United States vs Bosnia: Team News and Predicted Lineups For The Round of 32 Battle - Last Word On Football
news_items.author: Steen Kirby
news_items.text:
United States vs Bosnia: Team News and Predicted Lineups For The Round of 32 Battle - Last Word On Football

## Sports. Honestly. Since 2011

June 30, 2026 By Steen Kirby Featured, International Football, Starting Line-ups, USMNT, World Cup

Underdogs Bosnia will line up against an in-form United States squad on Wednesday in San Francisco. We offer team news and projected lineups for both teams, as the winner will be set for a historically strong World Cup performance.

Read More: 2026 World Cup Round of 32 Predictions Including USA vs Bosnia

## United States vs Bosnia

Date: Wednesday, July 1st, 2026 Venue: Levi’s Stadium in San Francisco Kick Off: 17:00 local time in San Francisco Referee: Raphael Claus Current Odds: United States 1.4, Draw 4.6, Bosnia 7.5

### United States Team News

After victory in the group stage, including a thumping defeat of a Paraguay side that upset Germany to reach the last 16, it’s the money rounds for the USMNT. Mauricio Pochettino’s men have shown a world class attack, scoring eight goals in three games, though the defence was significantly worse when Pochettino rotated his XI against Turkiye on matchday 3.

The key attackers Christian Pulisic and

The USA will look to lay another marker down on their home World Cup in Santa Clara on Wednesday night, when they face Bosnia and Herzegovina in the Round of 32.

Mauricio Pochettino's side won their group with 4-1 and 2-0 wins over Paraguay and Australia respectively in their first two games, which guaranteed that they'd top the group. Pochettino made a raft of changes for an inconsequential third group game against Turkiye, which the USA lost 3-2, after Kaan Ayhan scored the winner with the last kick of the game.

The likes of Tyler Adams, Folarin Balogun, and Chris Richards will return to the side for this game, as the USA look to book a Round of 16 tie against either Belgium or Senegal.

The United States believes in its soccer team again, and Mauricio Pochettino’s team is out to further embolden American dreams when it faces Bosnia & Herzegovina in the round of 32 at this summer’s World Cup.

Anxiety permeated across the country in the lead-up to the USMNT’s opening group game against Paraguay, but fears of a home humiliation were rapidly dispelled. Instead of wilting, it rose to the occasion and produced the most exhilarating performance of Pochettino’s tenure to date.

A 2–0 win over the plucky Australia followed, guaranteeing top spot in Group D with a game to play. That’s why the USMNT manager went on the defensive in the wake of the last-gasp defeat to Türkiye, with Pochettino opting for wholesale rotation to prepare for the knockout stages.

The U.S. must now bypass European opposition to prolong their World Cup journey, having historically struggled against UEFA members. Bosnia & Herzegovina is far from a juggernaut, but it’s a tall and physical team that has dominated from set-pieces at the tournament so far.
